Identity.Name: StingPressureRegimes
Identity.Definition: Clinical-room pressure regime per HTM 03-01 / SHTM 03-01. Defines the design pressure differential to surrounding spaces — drives airflow balancing, door undercut sizing, AHU sizing in MEPBalancingEngine, and the live pressure-regime audit. Each regime carries a documented design target (e.g. +25 Pa for operating theatre, -15 Pa for negative isolation).

Governance.StandardsBasis: HTM 03-01:2021 (Specialised ventilation for healthcare premises); SHTM 03-01 (Scotland); CDC 2005 isolation room guidance

Value.Definition: Positive +5 Pa — minor positive cascade; treatment rooms, clean-up rooms above corridor.
Positive +15 Pa — standard positive clinical room (ward isolation reverse, clean utility).
Positive +25 Pa — operating theatre standard per HTM 03-01.
Positive +45 Pa — ultra-clean orthopaedic / bone-marrow transplant rooms.
Negative -5 Pa — minor negative cascade; dirty utility, sluice rooms.
Negative -15 Pa — standard negative isolation per HTM 03-01.
Negative -30 Pa — full aerogenic infection isolation; high-consequence pathogen.
Neutral — design pressure equal to surrounding zones. Standard wards, offices.
Cascade — intermediate buffer zone. Pressure determined by neighbouring zones (e.g. anteroom between corridor and negative isolation).
Operating Theatre — alias for +25 Pa standard; carries OR theatre-specific commissioning requirements.
Protective Isolation — alias for +45 Pa; bone-marrow transplant / immunocompromised. Protects patient from external pathogens.
Source Isolation — alias for -15 / -30 Pa; protects others from patient (TB, MERS, smallpox).
Anteroom — buffer space adjoining an isolation room. Pressure between corridor and isolation pressure.
